review_text:
Great park and layout . Only complaint
Filters on the Dryers are not cleaned out each morning

I was hesitant writing this review however seeing the other recent reviews, and the replies from management I feel I must speak up. During the recent Easter long weekend, the male ablutions were disgusting. If they say they were cleaned at 10 every day this is an out and out lie. For them to not acknowledge how dirty their ablutions were, unfortunately speaks volumes for the management. Now let’s not even mention the fact there are no bins near camp sites. Other caravan parks manage this just fine but apparently it’s too hard for this one.  They seem happy to take everyone’s money but not to accept constructive feedback and make improvements.  It’s a shame. The location is amazing but management lets this park down. Better of finding somewhere else nearby and give this one a miss until they change their attitude or owners.

Absolutely appalling experience.

We booked and paid for 4 adults, but due to unforeseen circumstances—two members of our group being unable to travel from Europe because of the Iran war—we notified the park via email ahead of arrival. No response.

When we checked in, we raised it again, expecting at least some understanding or flexibility given the situation. Instead, we were met with a blunt, repeated reference to “terms and conditions” and zero willingness to even consider a partial refund or credit. No empathy, no attempt to help—just a cold, scripted response.

What made it worse was the attitude. When I suggested a simple compromise like a credit, it was dismissed outright. Then, incredibly, we were asked if we even wanted to stay, as they’d “have no trouble rebooking the site.” Keep in mind—we wouldn’t get any refund anyway, so that wasn’t exactly a real option, was it?

We still stayed, of course, but the principle here is what’s frustrating. The extra ~$80 we paid was purely for additional people—people who never arrived. The site didn’t cost the park anything extra, yet they were more than happy to pocket the money with no flexibility whatsoever.

I fully understand cancellation policies—they exist for a reason. But this wasn’t a last-minute cancellation of the booking itself. We still used the site. This was simply a change in circumstances, and a reasonable request for fairness was met with rudeness and indifference.

Customer service matters, and in this case, it was completely lacking. Would not recommend.

As for the stay itself, it’s frustrating because the location has so much going for it. It’s a great spot with awesome walking tracks and MTB trails, and the sites themselves are a good size. However, it would have been nice to be told in advance that a 20m power cable was needed for our site—something pretty basic that could easily catch people out.

Unfortunately, the amenities let the place down badly. The toilets were disgustingly dirty the entire time we were there. The urinal stank and barely looked like it had been cleaned, and the floors were consistently filthy. You’d think with such strict “terms and conditions” that seem happy to extract money for nothing, they could at least invest in a cleaner to keep the facilities in a decent, hygienic state.

Edit: At no point were we aggressive. Frustrated yes but aggressive no. A full refund for cancellation was never offered however it’s interesting you now say this was offered, in contradiction to your terms and conditions. So you could have offered a credit on compassionate grounds but chose not to. FYI, Karri Valley Resort offers full refunds within 3 days of cancellation and leaves it open for credit etc depending on circumstances. I emailed you two weeks ago to advise a change and you didn’t reply. That picture of the urinal was taken on day 4. It looked like this morning, noon and night as did the floors every day including the day we arrived before Good Friday. Perhaps think about cleaning twice a day in high volume periods.

Edit 2: so many recent reviews regarding the disgusting state of amenities yet they fail to acknowledge and apologise. Gives you an insight into how this park is managed. Avoid at all costs.

A fantastic spot to stay with everything you need in walking distance. The walking and mountain bike trails are super close and very easily accessible from the park, Pemberton Pool is just next door, and a short stroll sees you in the centre of town with the pub, tram, IGA, markets, etc. The team at the caravan park are so lovely to deal with especially Michala. The grounds are very well kept with a variety of shady and spacious sites. The kitchen and bathrooms are clean and tidy. We were so impressed we've already booked for next year. Although the caravan park was completely booked out and very busy during our stay it still felt peaceful and relaxing.

Terrible woman in the office. Very rude. Despite us explaining we were two adults short due to the Iran war, no refund or credit was given due to policy. We just got told a Perth couple cancelled and they didn't get a refund. We will never stay there again. Toilet block was dirty and never got cleaned while we were there for four nights. Water hose didn't reach the caravan and people had to let grey water run on the ground as the were no drainage holes for them.

So many small issues that add up. At $50 for an unpowered small tent site on the Easter Monday, you’d expect that the surcharge is to cover additional cleaning for the extra customers… sadly no! The toilets and communal areas weren’t visited by staff the entire time of our stay and were filthy for the morning rush. The camp kitchen garbage bins were full and tables dirty. They have some lame excuses for making patrons walk their rubbish to the bins instead of using some of their cash stock pile to purchase a golf cart or side-by-side to do cleaning duties. The shower/toilet blocks are in good working condition but reminiscent of the early 70s - small and basic. Camping sites are small and campers are packed together in chronological order (aka everyone in one end while there’s much
space at another - every other park spaces people out for comfort). Small issues but for the price, you’d expect better.

Amenities block was filthy the whole time we were there.  Some showers did not work properly and the BBQs weren't much better.  Lack of lights around the toilets and lack of bins.  The only thing going for this park is the bike/walking tracks nearby.

Fluorescent green lights and a high pitched continuous noise from a sodium chloride shower at the “Pump Hill Farm Cottages” (some run down power station looking buildings) around 25m from our tent site, has so far ruined any chance of sleep and rest… Bathrooms are foul - no one minds old or dated bathrooms, but these are filthy and unacceptable… Been here 2 days and they haven’t been cleaned yet - dirt, leaves, hair, toilet paper on floor. Would not stay here again.

We stayed at the Pemberton Caravan Park on 24th March 2026.
Thanks so much to Michael for your friendly and helpful service, and to everyone who maintains the park so beautifully. We loved the spacious, shady bays and the unspoiled natural surrounds. The camp kitchen was well set out and spotlessly clean. It's also one of the few parks we've stayed at with generous room in the showers - no soggy clothes and plenty of shelving and hooks to keep things organised!
I know a lot of visitors expect "bells and whistles" in caravan parks but we loved and appreciated the beautiful environment and excellent facilities. It was a highlight of our campervan trip, and we are definitely looking forward to going back.

Great location right in town. Staff were amazingly helpful and friendly. Full of advice for us and our dogs on where to visit. Van sites are ok and as you would expect. What drops the star rating is the age of the toilet/shower facilities are definitely showing their age with broken windows, broken toilet roll dispensers, lots of cobwebs. Did not see any facilities for the disabled either. Must say that there was plenty of hot water. I think it just needs some investment and modernisation which you see in other sites around the south of WA.
Nice park brilliant location 👍
Nice caravan park. Seem to have small amenities blocks for the size of the park, but were clean.

Me and a mate rocked up with minutes to spare and got an absolute beautiful spot for a camp.
Lady at the desk was lovely. I've stayed here before and it's just a lovely spot in a lovely town.
It is a popular camp ground so expect some noise at night by happy travellers but it's expected we are on holiday!
Tucked away in a little valley,  this caravan park is rustic and beautiful.   Great value and close to everything.   A±++++++
One of the best parks we've stayed at. Great surroundings.

위험해보이는 부분이 있어 적습니다,

1. 키친에 그릴은 가스불입니다, 약 5개의 점화불이 있는데 누군가 2개의 불을 켜놓고 그대로 갔어요,
저는 밤늦게 우연히 지나가다가 보게되어 껐어요,
그릴이 두껍고 커서 가스불이 직접 보이지 않아요, 그래서 누군가 불끄는걸 깜빡한 것같아요,

2. 밤늦게까지 아이들이 이렇게 노는 곳은 긴 캠핑장 여행중  유일했어요, 밤늦게까지 아이들이 뛰어노는 것이 방해가 되지는 않았습니다, 다만 뒤에 계곡에
물고기를 잡으러 밤 10시가 넘는 시간까지 있었어요, 전혀 불이 들어오지 않는 곳이라 위험해보였어요,

이 부분만 빼고는 다른것은 좋았어요
Really cool place to stay

Freundlicher Naturbelassener Campingplatz. Küche gut eingerichtet. Ältere, aber saubere Duschen. Gut möglich die Ortschaft zu Fuss zu besuchen.
Ebenfalls das öffentliche natur Schwimmbad. Wandern vom Platz aus sehr gut möglich.
Ich würde den Platz wieder besuchen.

Very central location in Pemberton, which makes it easy to walk into town. A big plus is that you can book online and choose your own site, which is really convenient. Some sites are very spacious, while others are quite small, so it’s worth checking the map when booking.
The camp kitchen looks new and is nice to use. The toilets and showers are fine, but some of the older buildings could use a bit of renovation.
Overall, a good and convenient place to stay in Pemberton.
A beautiful site to stay. Lovely staff however they could put a few extra rubbish bins around the site & the shower could be improved. Otherwise a lovely experience. Thank you

Nice situation.  Kitchen facilities looked good.  Never used as very busy with everyone using the Kitchen in park.
Toilets are little shabby and look half finished, eventhough they have been built for years.  One block looked like the floor hadn't been washed in months.

Geweldige plek! Mooie uitgangsbasis voor wandelingen en een geweldige openbare natuurlijke zwemgelegenheid aan de camping vast is heerlijk. Zeer vriendelijke en behulpzame staf. Alleen hebben de voorzieningen zoals kraan in de campkitchen, wasmachines en toiletten en douches wat aandacht nodig.

me and my family stayed here for one night and the neighbouring caravans sewerage was puddling near the taps and reeked, we couldn’t sit under outside because of the smell. gorgeous trails and pool though.

Great campsite with lots of different options depending on what you want to do and how you camp Also there is the Pemberton swimming pool within a walkable distance and that is definitely something you should go and visit. This campsite is fully equipped with kitchen and showers and toilets overall very clean and good campsite.
Beautiful camping spot for families!

Beautiful campground! They got me a spot with my beloved picknick table - it's all about the small things. If you're on the Bibb walk, just keep in mind it's a bit out of town. But by now, your feet can deal with that 😉

Beautiful setting amongst the trees with plenty of wildlife around the park.  Overall the place has a comfortable feeling and it's close to many of the tourists spots around town.  However, it also feels very tired.  Being a senior I need a seat to sit on to dry and dress myself and could not find one in the ablutions close to my camp-site.  The ablutions are not suitable for anyone with mobility issues and need to be updated to accommodate those people.  Also, there are no bins around the park and it's a very large park.  The only bins are near the entrance to the park. Also, the bays are quite narrow and difficult to have a van, annexe and car side by side.
However, the park is tidy and clear of rubbish and a good place to stay for a holiday.
I understand it is a community managed park and they're doing a good job keeping it going for the town.

Booked 2 nights camping and had a fantastic spot next to the creek.
Local parts visited every meal time and family of kookaburras kept us entertained with their songs and antics.
This is a great park. Large sites and plenty of amenities like a camp kitchen. Hot showers and laundry available.
Great park and staff. Amenities dated but work fine. Level sites and shady. Dump point. Good base camp for all the drives.
Beautiful scenery at this caravan park. Clean and tidy and lovely staff. Always a good experience when we stay

Really good location for exploring the forrest, close to bakery pub, main street. We had a van site with power and water, birds were almost over friendly when trying to eat outside :)
Didnt use the ammenities much as we were self contained. Toilet blocks not fancy but clean.

What a beautiful caravan park, easy access for big and small vans, good size camp spots, clean and well maintained.||The park is easy walking distance to every thing.|Easy access to amazing mountain bike trails and some interesting walking trails.

Spent 2 nights at this park with spacious sites,amenities clean but dated, pet friendly. Did a $35.00/ person tram ride from Pemberton to the Cascades, lots of good information from tram driver. Do not go to nearby sports club,didn't feel at all welcome there.

Our absolute favourite campsite! So lucky to get the spectacular spot by the stream. So near the town, was packed and bustling (in Oct), felt entirely safe and comfortable. Plus had an unforgettable experience feeding the ducks and parakeets!! (feed can be bought at the office for $2) Loved how it has direct access to he hiking trails too!
Beautiful location. The park is calm, clean and quiet. Surrounded by gorgeous trees. Facilities clean. Park staff friendly. Highly recommended.

Most people have really liked this place and we were no exception. Don’t take notice of the odd (very!) naysayer complaining about the toilets/showers. They’re a bit dated yes, but perfectly ok. Showers powerful and hot, cubicles are quite large and practical - your clothes & towel will keep dry! Site people very welcoming. Lovely forest walks out the back (kids mountain bikes catered for too) alongside a great natural swimming pool. Short drive/walk to the Pemby Tram trip and local shops & cafes. Highly recommended!
Ottimo campeggio con piazzole ampie e curate. Bagni in ordine: puliti e funzionali...

We had a powered site with plenty of room for our car and van. It rained heavily for most of our stay so the park was  water soaked.
The amenities are old however were kept clean and stocked. As a tall man could I suggest a more prominent sign for the low doorway into your bathroom. I smacked my head twice and yes I saw the sign after smacking my head going in and out.
The park is in a lovely spot and a close walk to town.
One final suggestion would be to place some bins close to the camp area rather than having to walk up the hill to your large bins. See pics not a bin in sight.

Lovely little caravan park right in town and next to the Pemberton Swimming hole/pool. It would have been nice if the caravan park had a few rubbish bins throughout the park apart from just the ones at the entrance. Apart from that, it’s great!
The toilet are so bad there is a smashed window small doors it has not been clean in years it looked like it was so bab for
Our site flooded very poor drainage otherwise nice park

What a dive this place is in the wet months , ankle deep in water and mud the moment you step out of your van on most sites , regardless of if it was raining or not , clearly inadequate drainage , spoke to a couple|That were there 15 years ago and was a problem back then aswell so would appear that it it’s never been addressed, campers kitchen was good , however a lack of waste bins throughout the park was evident, toilet facilities were likely ok when built decades ago but are pretty rough now , we are in a country that values water , however I’d think the wait for hot water would have been approx 60-70 litres before the hot water comes through , it’s my understanding that this is a community owned park , if so the local council really need to lift there game , a lovely town being let down by this facility , a pet friendly park but no bags to dispose of waste unless you bring them in yourself, place was that bad we left a day early on a three day stay
Nice, quite but bins too far away and camp kitchen could be cleaned more often. As destination wrong time of year to visit, as lots closed for break.
Rubbish bins? The only bins we could find were in the camp kitchen and marked "only for camp kitchen scraps".

Beautiful caravan park, lively man Mike was very friendly and helpful.
Would recommend to anyone

We stayed here during August 2019 in a powered site for four nights as a convenient base for day trips to surrounding areas (Busselton, Cape Leeuwin, Jewel Caves). It was very quiet, wasn’t crowded then and when booking in the manager said we could take our pick of the sites. The facilities were very clean and park fees were quite reasonable. I’d stay there again.

Beautiful little spot to stop for the night while driving southern WA.
Peaceful and relaxing.
We loved our stay here. The hosts were so friendly and accommodating. Such a welcoming environment in a great location. Definitely recommend!

Stayed once before, was good, Just old amenities, but Read the other negative reviews first, the T&C have made for a lot of upset customers regarding cancellations for less than 14 days, they are very firm and inflexible. More concerned about retaining a fee than having return customers. Consider elsewhere if that’s a problem. Otherwise enjoy the location.

Another really nice caravan park in regional Western Australia, on the outskirts of a really lovely little town. Pemberton has a remarkable range of eating, shopping and tourism opportunities and I really enjoyed my time there.
The Caravan Park is very well run, and set in amongst trees near the major hiking and biking routes. I stayed in the Blue Wren Cottage and it was lovely, well presented and with everything I needed. The communal laundry and kitchen were good, and new washing machines were in the process of being installed.
The Park is about a 15 minute walk from the centre of town, although an excellent bakery and lunch bar are situated quite close to the Park.
The staff were easy to deal with and responsive to any request.

Great location close to town but surrounded by forest. Right next to the swimming dam and mtb trails. Friendly welcoming staff. Fire pits and wood delivered to your site is a bonus. Plenty of room too - you can book the exact spot you want. Some of the facilities are old (but built to last!) and all are well maintained. Pemberton is a great little town too, highly recommend a visit.

Great local community park.  Good size and level sites.   Plenty of shade from surrounding trees.   Old amenities that really could do with an upgrade but serviceable and clean.
Free entry to the historic swimming pool next to the park.
Dump point on site.
I recommend the historic tram ride into the surrounding Rain Forrest with a really informative commentary
Don’t miss a cracking pie at the bakery opposite the park on the Main Street.
Pub serves great meals with cold beer.
opposite the pub is a caravan parking area.

The perfect place to base yourself to check out the amazing attractions nearby. The laundry and ablutions blocks are fairly dated but are quite presentable. We preferred the overflow non powered area for self contained RV’s as the park proper has lots of trees making solar recharge of our battery impossible.

Pemberton itself was a lovely surprise with a fabulous bakery, bike trails, a pool and pretty surroundings, and the service at the CVP made it even better.
We arrived on an overcast Thursday and asked for an unpowered site (save money to spend on caramel eclairs at the bakery, lol). The unpowered site we were given was terrible for solar, it was completely in the shade. Alas, we went back to reception and asked whether we could go to the overflow area (a large grass paddock behind reception, with very little shade). We were told that the boss could make that decision tomorrow and in the meantime, we were sent to a powered site that had good sunlight.
We ended up in the overflow area for the next four nights and it was wonderful. Quiet and bright, lots of sunshine for us to charge our house battery.
Sadly, we didn't see the resident Quokka that had been gracing the town's fb page but it was still a lovely stopover and I'd be happy to stay there again.
Such an epic caravan park, awesome facilities and absolutely fantastic camp staff. Had an amazing 2 nights here and will be back

We visited Pemberton while travelling through , we initially booked 1 day thinking this would be a short stop over.
After setting up camp and the vibe we felt we ended up staying 5 nights.
The sites are large and easy to reverse into.
Plenty of trees for shade and large green open spaces to enjoy.
There are different amenities blocks located  throughout the caravan park , a little dated but was very acceptable for a country caravan park.
We really enjoyed our time at Pemberton Caravan park and everything Pemberton has to offer. We thoroughly recommend you consider this town as a bit of R&R while travelling..
Quiet and peaceful. Close to a public pool and mountain bike trails. Grassy sites. Average facilities.
We had a lovely powered site by the river. The campsite is in walking distance to walking trails, the local swimming pool (lake) and the town centre.

Rooms got 2 because I'm in a tent.  Tents don't get a 5.
Close to town . Lunch at hotel waiting for check in. Good 👍

“Now this is a caravan park!”

Alright mate, I’ve seen my fair share of campgrounds, from the dusty outback to the lush bushlands, but this place? It’s a real beauty. You roll in, and straight away, you know you’re in good hands. Plenty of space to park up the van, no need to wedge yourself in like a croc in a billabong.

The amenities? Spot on! Showers with actual water pressure (none of that dribbling nonsense), barbecues that get hot enough to sear a decent steak, and a camp kitchen that’s cleaner than a dingo’s conscience—if they had one.

Now, the wildlife’s a real treat. Saw a few cheeky kookaburras eyeing off my brekky, and I reckon I heard a possum having a wild night in the trees. But don’t worry—nothing that’ll try to drag you off in the middle of the night.

Best part? The locals. Everyone’s up for a yarn, always ready to lend a hand if you’ve got a flat tyre or need a cold one. You don’t get that kind of hospitality just anywhere.

So if you’re looking for a place to pull up, kick back, and enjoy the great outdoors, this caravan park’s got my stamp of approval. Five stars, easy. Wouldn’t be surprised if they start calling it Crocodile Dundee’s Favorite Campground!
Beautiful spot but unfriendly staff…

조용한 캠핑장~ 개수대와 화장실이 구역별로 있어 좋았습니다
아침 새소리를 좋아하지만 이상한 소리를 내는 새때문에 기분 나쁘게 깼던 기억이 납니다
밤에는 정말 많은 별들을 볼수 있는 곳이기도 합니다
여기를 오시는 분은 꼭 mtb를 해보시길 추천합니다
옆에 있는 수영장에서는 가재도 만나골수 있을겁니다

Our son and daughter in law booked a site and us asked to stay with them for a couple of nights, when we got there the manageress was so rude and would not listen to what was being said, it was for a ford transit van with a tent on the side and a gazebo all fitting on one site, admitted it was for two people but all we wanted to do was pay for two extra, she would not even let us finish explaing and insisted on charging for a extra site that we did not use. However the site and facilities were quite good. Very disappointed with the manageress.
We stayed in our caravan, great location with bike trails out the back gate. Nestled in amongst tall trees and small creek running behind the park.
Neat clean tidy Caravan Park.Little ducklings where a treat to watch as are the beautiful trees, with lovely service at office.

Not sure what amenities people are saying are old and outdated. The one time sitting in writing this is fresh and clean. The sites down by the creek may be closer to camping than a flash fancy park but are spacious and have power and water. Maybe it's my kind of park without tribes of kids running and riding around and yuppy families everywhere. The bloke in the office was really friendly, helpful and welcoming of us and our dogs.
Top spot.

One of the nicest caravan parks on our journey though W.A. - situated in the beautiful Pemberton township - a rare gem. Very friendly proprietors. I left an item of clothing in the amenities block and they kindly arranged to have it posted to me in S.A.

Even with the park three quarters occupied, it is a very quiet place lending itself to you being able to relax. ||Ideally located within easy walking distance of the town centre. ||Love the surroundings of bush and being able to venture off on many of the designated walk tracks. ||Check in was friendly and informative. ||We would certainly stay again. ||The bathroom blocks are in bad need of a good spring clean and need an upgrade would be nice.   ||A lot of water going to waste from taps that will not close off properly around the park.
A little misunderstanding from allocation but our neighbours turned out to be most generous and lovely people. Good location facilities are well maintained.
Hot showers. Clean grounds. Friendly staff.

Extremely poor!!
Allows you to book in an animal when you do th booking sheet but in the depths of the terms and conditions no pets allowed in "peak periods"  BEWARE . YOU CANNOT ALLOW BOOKING AND OVERIDE IT WITH A TERM AND CONDITION IN small font!!
They will not change their website.  You can book it in and they will take your money.  The charge $50 admin fee to refund you.

We had 2 nights, beautiful surrounds. Nice walks, easy walk into town for an early morning coffee. Toilet blocks are fine, great value and amenities. Had a chat with Jason. This is a community owned park who are not out to make big money.  Doing a great job. 👏

Pemberton is a great central location for this south west area to set your self for a few days and do day trips to the four points of the compass.  This caravan park is central to the small township very proud of is timber industry heritage.  The caravan park will suit your needs without all the bells and whistles of the more starred and expensive parks.  The sites are huge with lots of space between sites.  Some sites are grass but the majority are sandy.  The office is high up on the entrance with a down hill run into the caravan park.  There is a bitumen and gutter road circular around with camps spread either side as well as some down near the river bend.  The ablution blocks may not be the most modern however they are practical, operational, clean and functional which serves your purpose well.  This is one of the rare caravan parks that allow you to have a camp fire so long as it is up off the ground.  The park has many fire places spread throughout the park that you can move to your camp site and sell wood if required.    There are numerous things to fill your day in near and within close proximity to Pemberton and this park is ideally suited.  The staff were polite efficient and friendly on our visit.  The park had a chilled vibe as arrivals are spread over the entire park and not packed in like sardines.  I really liked the spot and would happily return.
PS.  Feed the ring neck parrots once and they will be all over you.  Very friendly.  Night time a southern brown bandicoot would explore our campsite right up to us.

Caravan park doesn't have any solo bins around the park. You have to walk up a slite hill to skip bins.
On saying for people that are unable too it's an good Have to come across a park yet that does not supply bins.

Great location in beautiful surroundings, but the facilities (toilets & showers) could do with an update but still do the job so I suppose it adds to charm of the place. Could also do with the odd rubbish bin around the place instead of only one by the exit.
The park is filled with very tame birds who will eat out of your hand.

Just had an overnight stay in our rented 8m Motorhome at Pemberton.
We were woken in the early hours by a tree fallen over and landed on the roof!
We rang the afterhours number on the office door and Jason answered and came out with ladder, tools & chainsaw.
In no time out all he had chopped up the tree safely to get the camper van out from underneath.
He then repaired the broken skylight sufficiently enough for us to continue our holiday.
Bloody amazing service from Jason.
Can't thank him enough!
Great park and super helpful and friendly staff.
All good

Very friendly and helpful staff. Very overpriced for the standard of the amenities. Happy to pay $50 for a powered site, but would expect much more modern and better maintained amenities for that price.

Lovely spot and the nature around is amazing. We had a fire pit every night which is the whole point we picked the park. The staff were lovely too. However the amenities toilet and showers are terrible.
They are in desperate need of TLC. No bench in the shower to put clothes or help get dressed. They are old and dirty, all open which in winter is freezing. We found ourselves using public toilets while out to avoid the caravan park. If these were redone this place would be 5 star absolutely loved it beside the extremely run down showers and toilets.

Great park with selection of sites shady, sunny, small or large. The most obliging and helpful staff; after we departed they searched our site (at office closing time) and to our relief they located a small part from our camper trailer and offered to mail it to our home without charge. Will stay again, highly recommended.

Great location but !
This park needs some real maintenance on the amenities, the areas are poorly looked after .
Camp kitchen is open to the elements . Big disappointment as they push the community park element , definitely not worth $41 a night .
Beautiful park with lots of space. The only drawback is that it doesn’t provide Wifi.

We had such an amazing time here. The toilets, showers and kitchen area were so clean with running hot water. It’s a shame that we only stayed one night. The staff here were amazing and so friendly. Can’t wait to come back soon :)

Very neat and tidy, this park has come along way over the years! Since the new management has come along it really has come from quite a run down park to quite the opposite.

All the amenities are clean and in perfect working order, nice hot showers with plenty of water pressure.

The campers kitchen has everything that you'd expect, gas bbqs are provided and all kept of the best of condition...

The site are spacious amd locely green grass is in abundance, the old muddy sites along the river/creek are in excellent condition with appropriate road base being laid to allow the ground to handle the traffic without deterioration.

They allow dog in of peak times.

Pemberton is a beautiful town to visit and staying at this park is highly recommended, it has all the amenities that one coule need regardless of how your travelling but still offers the unique experience of feeling like your camping among the karri forest.

*Edit

Just spent a few days here with a large group, as usual we absolutely loved our stay!

All the amenities are clean and maintained, nice hot showers!

During winter they allow fires (big tick for our family)

Plenty of room on our sites (sites 10 - 15 all had vans)

Can't wait to get back!

Cheers guys!

It had been raining so much was wet, which probably makes giving a fair assessment a little difficult.  We were not given much choice in regard to sites, even though it was not busy at the time of our visit:  could easily have had a less rain affected site but not much flexibility and consideration shown therein.  Amenities were OK - some lighting issues though.  Good mobile phone coverage.||Loved the location.

I booked and paid for a 2 night stay for me and my dog. We were staying in my vehicle on an unpowered site. I stayed at the park before and had enjoyed it. As a repeat guest I expected the same again. But I was wrong. ||Our first night was peaceful but in the afternoon on the second day, the park manager booked 3 motorcyclists into the site immediately next to mine. I have a well behaved dog, but of course he reacted to the loud noise of the bikes.||It was clearly an untenable situation, so I was forced to leave the park because the manager had made a big mistake. He knew I had a dog but obviously didn’t care. ||I requested a refund for my second day, but the park owner refused, by citing their T&C’s that state “No refunds under any circumstances”. So, please be aware that this park has unfair customer relations and a “no refund” policy, even when their own staff are to blame. 🥲

Stayed at the caravan park. Friendly service.  Parked our van. Nearly set it up. And great no water. So had to move to other sandy site. Plumber had look at problem and they fixed it. Water pressure shocking. Sites very sandy. But location is good. Lots of trees and wild life. Toilets etc reasonably clean but old. Think the park just needs some attention and upgrade. Cheers

This is a bush style camping ground. I think there is only 1 cabin, but not sure as I haven't looked all around it yet.It is older but lovely. The amenities are also old, but goes with the atmosphere here. It is serene, plenty of birds and the trees are huge!! I love the place!!
Good location and clean and tidy park with old but clean showers and toilets.
Nice quiet, clean park with good size powered sites and amenities. Park has lovely trees and is close to many tourist features

Nicely located close to town. Very tight little streets in the park, which made manoeuvring large caravans very hard. To make matters worse, the park was completely booked the weekend we were there, so things were tight and others had to use the opposite site to back into theirs. Weird and old toilet blocks with communal hand basins. No bins located anywhere within the park - you take your rubbish up the hill to the big skip bins....not ideal when you need to take dog poo up late at night. Lady at reception when checking in was lovely.

We had to cancel our booking while on our way to WA from Sydney because our daughter was admitted to hospital in Sydney seriously ill and we had to return to Sydney from Ceduna to support her and her young family. The woman on the phone was abrupt and without any understanding or consideration.
We had paid in full $80 in January when we booked as part of our 50th Wedding Anniversary trip. The only refund she would offer was $30. No other caravan park had demanded full payment when booking.
Then when my wife’s emotions overcame her due to the circumstances, the woman on the phone accused my wife of being argumentative and hung up on her! I can’t believe people like this are the “public face” of a business! The people at every other caravan park where we had to cancel were all so kind and sympathetic. Most immediately offered a full refund of our deposit, even if they had a cancellation fee.
If we ever get the chance to get to WA, this is one caravan park we will avoid!

Never stayed in Pemberton and I can truly say what a lovely town and what a lovely caravan park. Stunning scenery, friendly people, very helpful staff (and thank you for getting my kids football down.) The kids had so much fun and made lots of new friends.
Must do for the kids would be the Aerial Adventure Pemberton and also a must to do would be the Pemberton tramway. We only spent 5 days here but enough to see what this town has to offer. Can't wait to stay again and bring our friends next time.
Need to be more specific about caravan length. Most places require total overall length. We ended up on a site for larger vehicles. This probably stopped someone booking in with a large vehicle.
Suitably impressed
Lovely quite CP. Friendly staff. Clean. As with most CPs now days, facilities, although very clean, are getting dated.

Schöner, ruhiger und idyllischer Campingplatz. Obwohl wir nach Ende der Büroöffnungszeiten ankamen hat uns der Manager (leider haben wir vergessen, ihn nach seinem Namen zu fragen) geholfen, unser Auto zu reparieren. Wir hatten ein Rücklicht verloren, es gottseidank wiedergefunden und er hat es außerhalb seiner Arbeitszeit so weit befestigt, dass wir am nächsten Morgen weiterfahren konnten. Vielen vielen Dank nochmal an unseren super freundlichen Helfer!!!!
We love our short stays away when we come for a visit

A quiet park surrounded by beautiful bush land with bike and walking trails. A short walk to town if you don’t mind walking up hills. You can also walk 150m to Pemberton Pool through a path from the park.  This park appears to have had some improvements done. The amenities are old but have been refurbished and the roads have tarred recently. I would stay here again.
Great place to stay and a really nice outdoor swimmingpool in a parc next to the campingsite.

Walking distance to the town, there is a local RSL for those who wish to eat out and most perfectly for us, the bike tracks were close by.

Great little spot with plenty of amenities

A beautiful location spoiled by lack of amenity cleanliness. The toilets and showers were not clean most of the time to the extent we drove into town to go to the toilets. I’m not a fussy person so now you know how bad it was
Nice park. Kids loved feeding the ducks and a short walk to the pool.

was booked in to pemberton park on 7/1/24 till 10/1/24,great little caravan park,great service,great location,quiet,peaceful and serene,but just one thing,the ablutions need a very serious upgrade,unfortunately they where dirty everyday,no privacy when on the toilet,doors too high,gaps from ceiling to toilet walls and the showers need a workout aswell,but besides that,the time we spent there was nice,if the ablutions get upgraded we will definately book again.6/10

Beautiful location, toilets and showers were disgusting for the 6 nights we were there, they were not cleaned by the look of things. Would not go back because of this problem.
Worst caravan amenities we have seen.
Campervan trip

Not a bad caravan park, not great but not bad. Had a great vibe and is in a fantastic location of Pemberton. The amenities quite old and in desperate need of some tlc. The toilet and shower closest to us appeared not to been cleaned over the 3 nights we stayed.

Great walking track to the nateral pool next door and loved the bird life around the park

The camp kitchen was a hub of activity, but ware your footwear as the prickles in the grass were dinamite

We would stay again, because of the location and the people we met

The owners were so helpful & friendly on arrival, which is a lovely way to be greeted.  The caravan park is in such a lovely location & is in walking distance into town & even closer to the Pemberton Pool . We had a large site with quite a distance between the next vans which made it feel so private & was a lovely quite park. The park is surrounded by beautiful old trees which are full of birdlife, we had regular visits from beautiful parrots , cockatoos,honey eaters, kookaburras, ducks   & wrens.  We even had a male Emu & his chick make an appearance
The toilets/shower blocks are older but were kept clean and tidy.
We stayed for 5 nights but we could have easily stayed longer & we were kept busy visiting the areas attractions . Will definitely make a return visit on our next lap .
Der Campingplatz ist echt cool..der Schwimmsee..war angenehm kühl..es hatte etwas Mücken...das Vogelfutter das man kaufen kann, lässt die Papageien tanzen..echt toll

Excellent caravan park! Spacious, quiet & very peaceful! Lovely friendly & helpful staff! Beautiful trees all around. Amenities kept clean & tidy, Camp kitchen excellent Nature walks lovely & cute pool nearby. Lots of ambience at this little gem of a park! Pemberton is certainly a worthwhile stay to explore!

Nous sommes rester plus de 3 semaines au Caravan Park, l’équipe est formidable

La cuisine est propre, il y a deux micro ondes, deux grilles pains, deux bouilloires, des plaque de cuissons et un frigo à disposition ainsi que des tables pour manger.

Les toilettes sont propre et les douche aussi, c’est laver quotidiennement.

Il y a des oiseaux et des canards qui se promene, la piscine de Pemberton est à 3 minutes à pied, le centre a 2 minutes.

Si vous travaillez dans les environs c’est un bon endroit pour camper.
One of the nicer caravan parks we have stayed in during our trip in WA. We enjoyed the ambience helped by the park not being too full.  Close to MTB Park too.

An amazing location, trees all around you, a very short walk to the Pemberton pool. A very nice spot to stay a couple of nights. The toilets/shower blocks were kept clean and tidy. The caravan park was quiet and we found the camping sites were quite large so our neighbours weren't right on our doorstep! such a lovely spot to stay for a couple of nights.
Lovely park but toilets a bit grotty and need updating.
Lovely caravan park set in tranquil location, close to town and other attractions.

We had the best time! My favourite time of day was feeding the ducklings and parrot's in the afternoon.  $2 from the office will get you a bag of seed. The park is clean, as is the little cabin on the hill. The park is just out of town centre and situated next to the Town Pool that is part of the river. Fantastic park for kids young or old as the bike tracks are also at the parks door step. Great location to get around the countryside to see so much. Top little place to stay.  Cheers Wendy
Picturesque location with amazing wildlife. Perfect for a family get away. Will be back again soon.
Perfect early spring trip!!
Great park in a bush setting, close to town. Plenty of walk and bike trails from east to difficult. Camp fires available
Perfect location, friendly service, hot showers and clean toilets, loads to see and do close to the park. Excellent cost per night for the powered sites. Will be back again

Great spot for camping. Pemberton pool just a stone's throw away. Creek running through the park.

Tidy ablution blocks and camp kitchen very roomy if you need it.

Spacious sites so you aren't breathing on your neighbour, and resident wildlife roaming freely.

Animals allowed outside of school holidays and long weekends

Enjoyed our time at this park,  good bush style camping. Some sites get very wet but wasn’t an issue for us. Toilets and shower blocks very ordinary from the outside but have had tastefully been upgraded inside.  Hot water took a long time to come through and water pressure isn’t great but all in all ok facilities and spotlessly clean.  Short walk through to the pool , central to the town and NPs. Our site was very sandy and we traipsed it in and out of our caravan the whole stay. Would ask for an all grass site next time !

We just spent a weekend at this tranquil little caravan park. The couple who run it are very friendly & accommodating. Plenty of toilet/shower blocks. Well appointed & clean Camp kitchen with a huge communal fridge. Plenty of bins available throughout & cleaned regularly. I highly recommend a van site down the back by the river.
Such a beautiful green place. X
Absolutely excellent caravan park in a stunning location just next to the Pemberton pool and mountain bike park
Would not recommended this business.they took $50 fee to cancel a booking made for September.
Fantastic camping experience, great kitchens and beautiful scenery
Very nice staff

We booked this place and within an hour of booking the lady told us we had to cancel because they couldn’t accommodate our booking with a $50 cancellation fee. BEFORE they had even accepted the booking or taken payment out. Would not recommend
